description_long: This is a simple wooden fence composed of three vertical posts and two horizontal rails, all with a rectangular cross-section. The wood appears to be unfinished or lightly finished, giving it a natural, light brown color. The top edge of the fence is flat, with the posts extending slightly above the upper rail, creating a stepped appearance at each post. There are no visible decorative elements or embellishments, and the construction is utilitarian, likely intended for demarcating boundaries in parks, gardens, or along sidewalks. The fence is solid and sturdy, with the rails securely attached to the posts. The overall design is minimalistic and functional, suitable for urban or suburban environments.
description: A simple, light brown wooden fence with three posts and two horizontal rails, featuring a flat top and no decorative elements. The design is minimal and functional, ideal for boundary marking.
description_view_0: Front view showing three vertical posts and two horizontal rails, with the posts extending above the top rail.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the rectangular cross-section of the rails and posts.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same structure as the front, with the posts and rails clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the alignment of the rails and posts.
